2026-04-23T15:14:11.5169172Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T15:14:11.5169596Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T15:14:11.5170142Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T15:14:11.5170456Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T15:14:11.5170696Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-23T15:14:11.5172741Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-23T15:18:08.0832096Z **Summary:** The push bumped versions for two `azurestackhci` packages. `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm` has 21 auto-generated sample test files covering all resource types (VMs, VHDs, gallery images, logical networks, etc.) but its README Examples section is nearly empty — just a generic link with no code snippets. The issue includes a full implementation guide for creating a `samples/Readme.cs`, adding snippet placeholders to the README, and running the snippet/API-export tooling to wire everything together.

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"name":"dispatch_triage"}]}}

Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The package covers VM lifecycle management on Azure Stack HCI clusters, including virtual machine instances, virtual hard disks, gallery images, logical networks, storage containers, network interfaces, and security groups.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The `README.md` Examples section contains only a generic link to `.NET Management Library Code Samples` — no package-specific code snippets whatsoever\n- The test directory `tests/Samples/` contains 21 auto-generated sample files (VM instance CRUD, VHD management, gallery image management, logical network management, storage container management, network interface management, security group management), none of which are surfaced in the README\n- No `samples/Readme.cs` file exists to back README snippet placeholders (unlike the sibling `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci` package which has `samples/Readme.cs`)\n- The `Key concepts` section is a stub linking only to generic SDK docs — no description of HCI VM-specific resource model (VmInstance, LogicalNetwork, VirtualHardDisk, etc.)\n- The package description in the csproj (`Azure Resource Manager client SDK for Azure resource provider Hci.Vm`) is generic and doesn't describe the service\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md`** — Edit\n\nReplace the `## Examples` section with the following (the snippet bodies will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after Step 2):\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm` lets you manage virtual machine workloads running on Azure Stack HCI clusters. The key resource types are:\n\n- **HciVmInstance** – A virtual machine instance running on a HCI node (child resource of an Arc machine).\n- **HciVmVirtualHardDisk** – A managed virtual hard disk stored on a HCI storage path.\n- **HciVmLogicalNetwork** – A logical (VLAN/overlay) network on a HCI cluster.\n- **HciVmStorageContainer** – A storage container (SMB share or local path) for placing VM disks and images.\n- **HciVmGalleryImage** / **HciVmMarketplaceGalleryImage** – VM OS images sourced from a local gallery or Azure Marketplace.\n- **HciVmNetworkInterface** – A virtual NIC attached to a logical network.\n\n## Examples\n\n### Authenticate the client\n\n```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_AuthClient\n```\n\n### Create a virtual machine instance\n\n```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_CreateVmInstance\n```\n\n### Get a virtual machine instance\n\n```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance\n```\n\n### List all virtual machine instances under a host machine\n\n```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_ListVmInstances\n```\n\n### Delete a virtual machine instance\n\n```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_DeleteVmInstance\n```\n\n### Create a virtual hard disk\n\n```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_CreateVhd\n```\n\n### Create a logical network\n\n```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_CreateLogicalNetwork\n```\n```\n\n**File: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/samples/Readme.cs`** — Create\n\nCreate this file to back the README snippet placeholders above. Use the patterns from `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/samples/Readme.cs` and the existing generated samples in `tests/Samples/` as a reference. A minimal starting point:\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Core;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm.Models;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm.Samples\n{\n    public class Readme\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task AuthClient()\n        {\n            #region HciVm_Readme_AuthClient\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task GetVmInstance()\n        {\n            #region HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            // The resource URI is the Arc machine resource ID that hosts the VM\n            string resourceUri = \"subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines/{machineName}\";\n            ResourceIdentifier hciVmInstanceResourceId = HciVmInstanceResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(resourceUri);\n            HciVmInstanceResource hciVmInstance = client.GetHciVmInstanceResource(hciVmInstanceResourceId);\n\n            HciVmInstanceResource result = await hciVmInstance.GetAsync();\n            HciVmInstanceData resourceData = result.Data;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"VM Instance id: {resourceData.Id}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task DeleteVmInstance()\n        {\n            #region HciVm_Readme_DeleteVmInstance\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            string resourceUri = \"subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines/{machineName}\";\n            ResourceIdentifier hciVmInstanceResourceId = HciVmInstanceResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(resourceUri);\n            HciVmInstanceResource hciVmInstance = client.GetHciVmInstanceResource(hciVmInstanceResourceId);\n\n            await hciVmInstance.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\n            Console.WriteLine(\"VM instance deleted.\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n\u003e **Note:** The `CreateVmInstance`, `ListVmInstances`, `CreateVhd`, and `CreateLogicalNetwork` snippets should be authored similarly, drawing from the existing generated samples in `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmInstanceResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmVirtualHardDiskCollection.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmLogicalNetworkCollection.cs`.\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n1. Write the sample code in `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/samples/Readme.cs` (created in Step 1) with `#region Snippet:\u003cSnippetName\u003e` / `#endregion` markers as shown above.\n\n2. Add snippet placeholders in `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md`:\n\n   ````\n   ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance\n   ```\n   ````\n\n   The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will replace the empty placeholder bodies with the actual code.\n\n3. Snippet names must match exactly between the test file region and the README placeholder:\n   - `HciVm_Readme_AuthClient`\n   - `HciVm_Readme_CreateVmInstance`\n   - `HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance`\n   - `HciVm_Readme_ListVmInstances`\n   - `HciVm_Readme_DeleteVmInstance`\n   - `HciVm_Readme_CreateVhd`\n   - `HciVm_Readme_CreateLogicalNetwork`\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md` is mostly structured correctly but needs these updates:\n\n| Section | Status | Action |\n|---|---|---|\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Stub | Replace with HCI VM resource model description (see Step 1) |\n| Examples | ❌ Empty | Add snippet placeholders (see Steps 1 \u0026 2) |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\n\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 azurestackhci` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from sample files and injects code into README.md placeholder blocks; verify with `git diff` that README was updated\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 azurestackhci` — only needed if public API surface changed\n5. `dotnet format sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/` — ensures consistent formatting\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[AzureStackHCI] Docs: Add code examples to Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm README"},"name":"create_issue"}}}
